title: Set Screen Time Limits
summary: Set daily time limits for specific apps or overall device use.
learnerPromise: You will be able to set daily limits for apps or overall screen time and understand how to adjust them as needed.
whyItMatters: Being intentional about screen time can help you focus on schoolwork, sleep better, and make room for offline activities. Setting limits puts you in control of your device use.
realLifeUses: You want to reduce time spent on social media during school days
You find yourself staying up late on your phone and want a bedtime reminder
You want to focus on homework without constant app notifications
You want to track how much time you actually spend on your device each day
materialsNeeded: Access to device settings or a screen time management app
result: App or device time limits are active in your settings and a daily report is visible.
youWillLearn: How to find the screen time or digital wellbeing settings on your device
How to set a daily time limit for a specific app or category of apps
How to schedule downtime or focus modes during certain hours
How to review your daily or weekly screen time report
miniChallenge: Set a daily time limit of your choice for one app and schedule a focus or downtime period.
steps: Open your device settings and find the screen time, digital wellbeing, or app limits section
Turn on screen time tracking if it is not already enabled
Choose one app or app category and set a daily time limit that feels reasonable to you
Set up a focus mode or downtime period for times when you want fewer distractions
Check your screen time report after one day to see how the limits worked
completionCriteria: You have set at least one app time limit or scheduled downtime period
You can view your screen time report and understand how you are spending time on your device
commonProblems: The limit is too strict and you keep overriding it — try a more realistic limit and adjust over time
Limits apply to the wrong apps — double-check which apps are included in each category
Focus mode blocks apps you actually need — customize the allowed apps list
tips: Start with generous limits and tighten them gradually as you build new habits
Use focus modes that allow important contacts or apps while blocking distractions
Set up a bedtime reminder so your device dims or notifies you when it is time to wind down
Review your screen time report weekly to notice patterns and adjust your goals
